mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment spike, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts near-term corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series expand, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals immediate economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3 percent annually forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising real discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 60-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate profitability, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal predicts negative returns in >55% of cases during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 95.0 index threshold indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness, which depresses earnings outlooks and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods when INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the threshold loses predictive significance in out-of-sample validation.
